_readme: Plan-map data. One entry per topic. Ten-second capture: append {id,title,campaign,status} — wire deps/links later. Viewer: /Aralia/planmap/index.html on any dev server. status: parked | specced | active | done. deps: ids this topic REQUIRES. A dep may also carry `feature: <slug>` to narrow the arrow to ONE feature of the target topic (slug = that feature's title run through the same slugifier as the ids); the `why` still explains the edge, the pointer is now structural. link: repo-relative spec doc path (opened via the dev server's /@fs bridge using repoRoot). Truth-ownership + promotion rules (who owns statuses/decisions/deps; gaps flow UP by human curation only, plan flows DOWN via planmap-to-wave): tools/agora/PLANNING-STACK.md. `open`: integer mirror of the linked subspec's '## Open' bullet count (recompute when the doc changes — it is NOT invented, it is counted). `spike: true`: research that could invalidate the approach (dashed ⚠ tile). status also allows 'superseded' (dead decision — drops to the Graveyard strip; add a one-line `killed` reason). `focus: true` on at most ONE topic = what Remy is actually touching now (★ FOCUS).

topics.title: Beautification wave
topics.sub: beauty first, perf later · anchor: BG3 · realism climbs everywhere

deps.why: CHOSEN, mixed-grain: only the TSL code lifts (trees/grass/wind/clouds/sky/GI from fable5-world-demo, MIT) hard-require the new renderer — props/catalogs are renderer-agnostic — but building every visual packet once, on the final renderer, beats building twice

Lighting/atmosphere migration

Full-catalog bridge flip (placement draws the 105-def catalog, density governed)
PropPlacementContext enrichment: tavern / wealthy / gate / ruin / riverbank / defile contexts fire
Scatter de-gridding: grass tufts drop dotted-row artifact (decorrelated jitter + patchy noise density)
Grass wind sway + close-up read polish
Second (far) shadow cascade for wide town overviews
Broadleaf canopy clamp (no roof-swallow near buildings)

topics.title: Building blueprint pipeline
topics.sub: blueprint-first: design the 2D blueprint, build 3D from ONE shared data shape (rooms/doors/walls/stairs in feet)

deps.why: Grows the L4 interior generator into the shared building-maker rather than forking a second one: it already emits pure InteriorPlan data (rooms/doorways/furnishings/stairs, feet-canon 5 ft) and feeds the 3D build — the pipeline extends that data shape. Reverse experiment 2026-07-05 (PreviewBlueprint) proved one shared renderer serves the 2D blueprint AND the 3D build both ways

Interior furniture IS props: beds, tables, hearths, counters, barrels place through the prop schema + placement engine (with referee data) — same system dungeon dressing uses, no prop system no furnishing
Walls carry real thickness on the grid line so combat reads cover: the blueprint's wall model feeds the fight-in-place 5 ft tile referee — a building interior IS a combat surface

features.title: Blueprint data contract (rooms/doors/windows/furniture/stairs/walls, feet)
Believable layout (room purposes, dominant main room, real corridors, sensible entrance)
Module-map 2D blueprint drawer (pure view; scale bar + room numbers)
Walls as real-thickness grid edges (cover for the referee)
Basements + multi-storey
Irregular footprints (L-shapes, wings, tower) raised in 3D

topics.title: Procedural dungeon generator
topics.sub: THEME SPINE + SHEET LOOK LANDED 2026-07-06 (after critique wave + feet-canon + compound rooms/elbow corridors): 3 themes (crypt/cavern/frost) each own palette, name pools, monster table, debris vocabulary + thematic exterior glyphs; drawer has title cartouche, brooding vignette, per-room floor tones; layout invariant across themes at the same seed (verified). 11/11 tests. Remaining: 3D scene builder (Dungeon Forge poach list saved), world attachment, real bestiary, rotation/verticality/symmetric wings || AMBITION APPROVED 2026-07-07: Remy green-lit all four pillars (history-first, world-grown, diegetic map+3D, multi-level) — kickoff prompt written for a new agent; history-first is the spine, build it first. || PILLAR 1 BUILT 2026-07-06: builder-archetype intact layouts + seeded decay events with map evidence + derived text (name/blurb/notes/rumor hooks) + 2D parchment sheet + sprawl dial; 72 tests green, final review READY; Remy iteration waves folded in.

deps.why: Mirrors the L4 interior generator's contract 1:1 — deterministic seed paths, pure-data POJO output, zero THREE imports, feet-canon 5 ft grid; interiors proved the pattern this reuses
Dungeon decoration IS props: pillars, torches, chests, braziers and set-dressing place through the prop schema + placement engine (with referee data) — no prop system, no dressing

Dungeon rooms are combat encounters: fights resolve through the fight-in-place 5 ft tile referee, and difficulty maps to a real D&D XP budget — the dungeon is the purpose-built home for in-place combat

features.title: Pure-data generator (growth layout: door → corridor → room, loop doors through wall gaps)
Acceptance test suite (determinism, 100% connectivity, boss depth, loops = cyclomatic, prop/spawn legality, perf)
2D top-down Dungeon preview step with overlay toggles (Delaunay/MST/loops/critical/heatmap)
Feet-canon boundary conversion (rooms/props/spawns to feet per DungeonPlan contract)
WebGPU/TSL 3D scene builder (buildDungeonScene: instanced geometry + baked lighting)
Decoration richness + real monster/CR sourcing from the bestiary
World attachment point (hidden-place leaf / building stair-down / travel destination)
History-first generation: build intact structure with purpose, then simulate decay events; notes/lore emitted from the true event log
World-grown dungeons: theme/monsters/name/entrance derived from atlas cell + nearby burg; entered from 3D wilderness or interiors

Reverse-generational world chronicle: infer history backwards from the present atlas (state borders imply past wars, religions imply schisms, ruins imply fallen predecessors); one chronicle feeds dungeons, rumors, and town lore
Diegetic map: same plan renders 3D level AND parchment sheet; sheet inks in with exploration (fog-of-war on the drawing)
Multi-level descent: stairs to deeper seedPath levels, pits, balconies; multi-page sheet, boss at the bottom

topics.title: Plan-map (this)
topics.sub: tech-tree roadmap: ten-second topic capture + dependency view

topics.title: WebGPURenderer migration
topics.sub: three.js WebGPU + TSL; unlocks MIT code lifts from fable5-world-demo; VALIDATED on the RTX 2070S (37fps headless, 15.5M tris)

features.title: WebGPU probe: streamed ground scene on WebGPURenderer
Material parity pass (terrain, water, buildings)
features.note: Root cause: R3F-mounted scene lights don't drive the WebGPU LightsNode (three #30044 / r3f #2853) -> zero irradiance -> black terrain. Fixed by baking hemisphere+sun Lambert into unlit MeshBasicNodeMaterial colorNodes (no scene lights). Verified on WebGL2-fallback node path; real-GPU eyeball still pending. Sky = fog+CSS backdrop (fine); postprocessing = backlog.

features.title: Probe full-stack parity
features.note: WHY (dep on the material parity pass): parity was proven only on flat terrain + building boxes while the ground world now ships trees, grass, the full prop set, styled roofs, walls/roads/decks. The probe (?phase=webgpuprobe) now renders the SAME full stack the live scene does at the same seed/pose, reusing the game's data (chunk bundles + GroundWorld.props) with the baked-TSL Lambert path. FAIL-FAST aligned with the battle map: pre-checks navigator.gpu + requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter OR a non-WebGPU backend -> NO scene, an error pane ('WebGPU unavailable: <reason>') with one explicit 'View this scene in WebGL instead' button (routes to ?phase=world3d&ground=1 at the same pose; user-driven, never auto-fallback). Green 'WebGPU' + live FPS badge only on genuine success. Documented MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen): real-time shadows on the node path (no LightsNode consumes a shadow map when lighting is baked into colorNode). Key node-path gotcha found + fixed: on InstancedMesh the InstanceNode multiplies instanceColor into the color pipeline UNCONDITIONALLY, so a dark per-instance scatter tint crushed lit trees/bushes to black — proven in isolation on a real WebGPU backend ([0,19,0] with dark instanceColor vs [109,158,88] without) and fixed by leaving instanceColor null (trees keep geometry leaf-green; bushes use a solid green). Headless capture correctly shows the error pane (no WebGPU adapter in headless Chromium); rendered WebGPU full-stack needs the real-GPU eyeball (RTX 2070S).

features.title: Battle-map + modal scenes migration
features.note: Battle-map WebGPU render path behind ?gpu=1 (WebGL default unchanged): BattleMap3D delegates to BattleMap3DGpuScene, same shared combat hooks/data, baked-TSL Lambert (no scene lights). FAIL-FAST preserved: probes navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter -> NO scene, error panel + one explicit 'Use WebGL instead' button (user-driven, never auto-fallback); gl factory throws on a non-WebGPU backend; WebGPU badge only on genuine success. VISUAL PARITY PORT DONE (2026-07-05, rungs 1-4 + rung 5 wired): (1) procedural terrain texturing TRANSLATED from the onBeforeCompile GLSL to a TSL node graph (gpu/terrainColorNode.ts: per-type palettes, FBM/voronoi noise, organic edge blend, slope-rock, wet banks, dapple) — not a flat palette; (2) instanced grass + ground scatter (mirrors GrassLayer/GroundScatter); (3) grid + movement/path/AoE overlay translated to TSL (gpu/gridOverlayNodes.ts), fade-lerped, terrain-conforming, BattleMap3D passes validMoves/activePath/actionMode/aoeSet; (4) tokens gained team color + HP fade + selection/active rings; (5) node PostProcessing bloom+vignette wired (dynamic-imported, real-GPU-gated). Node graphs unit-tested WITHOUT a GPU (5 new tests); battle-map suites 12/8 green; tsc clean. STILL MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen in a red list, no faking): real-time shadows (baked colorNode has no LightsNode for a shadow map), full animated CharacterActor rig + drei nameplates (1,491-line drei/AnimationMixer stack), GPU wind sway on grass. Captures: bm3d-port-webgl-ref.png (target look) + bm3d-port-webgpu.png (headless fail-fast pane, correct with no adapter). Rendered WebGPU battlefield still needs the real-GPU eyeball via ?gpu=1 (RTX 2070S). Modal (non-battle) scenes still parked.

SHIPPED same day (each tsc-clean at 635 baseline, travel suite green): ferry fares + affordability + gold deduction (G15); sea lane/coastal/open-ocean danger tiers + trip-level maritime encounter roll (G16); forced-march exhaustion via CON saves (G1, 9h=DC 11); navigation drift / getting lost with seeded time penalty (G2). dock tiers + tender legs (G14) shipped fully additive (dock size derived from burg population, world-gen untouched). ALL 5 real travel gaps done (G1/G2/G14/G15/G16) + owned-ship (G13); travel registry down to 2 open, both design-gated (G4 transport schema, G11 marker-merge contract). REMAINING: cosmetic readout labels (AtlasSvgView tender dotted line + travelReadout fare/tender text) + in-app visual eyeball of a live ferry/lost/exhaustion trip.

topics.title: Player-character aging
topics.sub: birthdate-derived age · perk bundle · non-lethal frailty floor · per-race age table

topics.title: Generational time — the world grows old with you
topics.sub: the world clock ages EVERYONE: NPCs gray, die, and are succeeded (the shopkeeper's daughter takes over the shop; households turn over; graveyards fill); the player ages per the aging spec and on death continues as heir/apprentice, with the old character's deeds becoming world history NPCs remember. One clock, pure rules, emergent legacy — no AI calls. Claude's pick 2026-07-06 when Remy asked for one dream feature outside combat; captured parked at Remy's request.

deps.why: the player-side aging spec is the foundation this generalizes to the whole population
NPC daily lives and relationships are what aging gives ARCS to; succession rides the sim's household model
living buildings give turnover somewhere visible to happen: the inherited shop, the emptied house, the new family moving in

Bigger battlefield + robust zoom + flowing water: 120x90 map (10.8k tiles), zoom 15-300% anchored at cursor/center, scroll-extent leak fixed (spacer clip), water painted as merged rounded blobs with feather + crisp sandy shorelines, square water tile-tint removed; road re-routed as an A-to-B west-east trail with small wobble + water dodging (was aimless sine squiggles)

topics.title: Procedural SVG combatant art
topics.sub: generate distinct combatant tokens + roster portraits as owned procedural SVG (no image assets, no reliance on the broken image-gen backend); Remy's chosen path over the 238 race images. Feeds the reskinned 2D battle map + party/enemy cards so tokens/portraits look like real characters, not letter-blobs. AMBITION EXTENDED 2026-07-06 (captured at Remy's pick): full painted top-down miniatures with idle motion, visibly bloodied at half health, visibly burning when on fire — drawn as sprites on the nextgen renderer's token layer

deps.why: The reskin left tokens/roster as letter-blobs and race images are not meant for tokens (Remy 2026-07-05); owned SVG generation is the chosen art track that fills that gap on the reskinned surface

topics.title: Shipped-on-timeline
topics.sub: done topics render inside their campaign lane on a shared elapsed-time axis; snap to history.built, then an observed done transition, otherwise an honest date-unknown bucket

deps.why: enhances the plan-map: retires the bottom Shipped strip into in-lane dated tiles

topics.title: Dev-server crash guard
topics.sub: dev servers no longer die on the Vite ERR_SERVER_ALREADY_LISTEN restart race — a crash-guard preload swallows the benign error, wired into every launch config

topics.title: Claude Design UI system
topics.sub: canonical claude.ai/design project (id aedb018f) mirrors the real Aralia UI — token sheet, 10 components, screen mockups, brand assets; auth unblocked via /design-login into shared ~/.claude credentials; combat-view card ADDED (refreshed to current CombatView.tsx). Rescue verification DONE: window-frame matches, merchant ~80% (now WindowFrame-framed + faction pricing), town STALE (2D tile retired → Worldforge SVG+3D), design-preview superseded by repo's own DesignPreviewPage (~50 panels). OPEN: which stale screens to port/refresh; old folder (id 4c0a555c) deletion is Remy's in-browser call (handover written 2026-07-05)

topics.title: Master orchestrator
topics.sub: one master session manages several orchestrators: roster → rollup → conflicts → act; steps in to adopt a dead orchestrator's orphaned campaign. Doctrine + master.mjs CLI + one guarded POST /campaigns/:id/adopt (mirrors unlock --force). Specced 2026-07-06 (approach B: doctrine + tools for a driver); not built

deps.why: The master’s roster and conflicts views are only legible once agents carry real identities (type/role/spawnedBy) — otherwise it is arbitrating over cryptic handles like agent-16d417

features.title: campaign adopt endpoint: POST /campaigns/:id/adopt, guarded like unlock --force, reopens tasks + emits event, with tests
master.mjs CLI: roster / rollup / conflicts / nudge / watch / adopt (composes existing endpoints)
MASTER-ORCHESTRATOR.md doctrine (above ORCHESTRATOR.md) + GLOSSARY entries
Codex-cli TODO sweep + marker cleanup
PK-iter2 smoke verification and iter task execution handoff

topics.title: Agent identity & provenance
topics.sub: Wave 1 BUILT + GREEN 2026-07-06 (store/server/client, 121 agora tests): type/spawnedBy/campaign/cwd/handleValid + validateHandle on registerAgent (on top of fable’s role) + whois/lineage/tree + clean retire (retireAgent + POST /agents/retire + `retire` cmd). Goes live on next daemon restart. Remaining: PROTOCOL.md doc + spawner stamping in orchestrate dispatch. Foundation for retrace/ping/escalation/master

features.title: Rich identity record: add type/role/spawnedBy/campaign/cwd to the agent + validate structured handles
Provenance views: whois <handle>, lineage (walk spawnedBy to root), tree (fleet spawn tree by campaign)
Stamping: spawner bakes identity into launch (orchestrate dispatch / Agent prompt); root sessions self-declare

topics.title: Agent retrace
topics.sub: Store-core BUILT+GREEN 2026-07-06 (125 agora tests): preserve-on-reap dossier (identity + filesHeld + sayTail + checkpoint) + reapCount + checkpointTask (latest-wins) in sweepExpired; a clean retire stays clean (no crash dossier). Remaining: `retrace <id>` command (dossier + git diff) + task checkpoint command/endpoint + successor-flag on claim. Depends on agent-identity (built).

deps.why: The dossier is only useful if it captures WHO died and where from — retrace stamps the dead agent’s full identity record, so a bare handle is not enough; identity is the prerequisite

features.title: Preserve-on-reap: build the retrace record in sweepExpired before freeing locks + dropping the agent
task checkpoint --did --next --files (latest-wins resumable note) + reapCount escalation
retrace <taskId> command: dossier + live git diff of files held + successor resume protocol

topics.title: Agent Matrix dashboard
topics.sub: local operator-dashboard control plane at localhost:3040: live dispatch tiles, agent offerings, external review monitor, PAT Vault links, and fetch-all probe supervision. Fetch-all checklist popout built 2026-07-05 so the operator can watch each live probe run down instead of reading one changing button label.

features.title: Fetch all live checklist popout: opens a draggable progress window and marks each probeable agent queued, running, complete, or failed as probes execute

topics.title: Agora operator dashboard
topics.sub: the human's window into the agent fleet at localhost:4319: live feed, presence, locks, reservations, task board — and now a two-way surface: message composer (codex, 2026-07-06) + operator display name so Remy posts as himself, not operator-dashboard-<hash> (fable, same day). Grew out of the codex-claude live-collab session

deps.why: dashboard posts are how the human pokes listening agents awake — the composer is only a two-way surface because the wake channel exists

features.title: Message composer: human posts to the Agora feed through the same authenticated path as client.mjs say
Operator display name: persistent, sanitized handle for dashboard posts (localStorage + re-register on change)
Fix daemon duplicate-handle acceptance: register with unique:true returned 201 for an already-held handle (promised 409)

Command channel: role-gated second feed — orchestrator/master/human may post, workers get 403; dashboard Command Feed panel with its own composer, @mention targeting (no dropdowns); say/inbox --channel; roles on register
File reservations tile: FIFO dibs queue for contested paths, enforced before lock acquisition, visible in dashboard, health, SSE, CLI, and protocol docs

topics.title: Agent ping / wake
topics.sub: poke an agent awake across runtimes, not just message it. PROVEN 2026-07-06 (Path C): a live Claude chat arms a persistent in-session monitor on the Agora inbox — any agent's say wakes it for a turn (Codex PING → Claude PONG, no human). Claude→Codex leg: Codex on Windows cannot hold a listener, so its wake = launching a fresh codex exec worker; the awake Claude is the actuator (Leg 2 PROVEN via PONG round trip). Wake-tag convention: listeners wake only on direct address or their @tag in a broadcast. Remaining: Path B resume test, standing switchboard for dormant handles

deps.why: ping routes by the target’s type + contact channel (claude-session vs codex vs live-SSE) — with no identity there is nothing to route on

features.title: Path C: in-session Agora monitor wakes a live Claude chat on any agent's say (Codex PING → Claude PONG round trip)
Leg 2: Claude wakes Codex by launching a fresh codex exec worker with the pending Agora message (PONG round trip proven)
ping <handle> on Agora: route by identity type — SSE push (live) + relaunch (headless codex/CLI)
Path B spike: does codex/claude same-session resume cleanly wake a desktop-owned thread without double-driving it?
master orchestrator nudge → ping (wake, not just deliver)

topics.title: Roads as real combat-map terrain
topics.sub: promote the painted A-to-B road to generator-level terrain: tiles under it become road (movement bonus), generator routes it, enables road-ambush encounter framing. Floated 2026-07-06 during the reskin road fix; not yet chosen.

deps.why: the reskin's painted road is the visual proof; this makes it mechanical

topics.title: Next-gen 2D combat map (one renderer, world-derived battlefields, living board)
topics.sub: three-pillar ambition chosen 2026-07-06: single-scene renderer replacing the 10.8k-DOM-tile grid; battlefields derived from the actual Worldforge cell; ambient life + cinematic turns + narrated log + sound. STATE 2026-07-07: D1 Pixi prototype BUILT (6 tasks reviewed, ?pixiboard=1). TEN biomes painted; forest + desert now REAL PAINTED ART (Caeora packs via a generic per-biome sprite-pack loader). Combat demo relocated to the design preview (?step=battlemap) + window-frame layout fixed; combat 3-col layout survives >=1024px. OPEN: cave still 'not believable' (fix via forge or a Caeora cave zip); Remy eyeball verdicts on water round 6 + biome looks; D1 final whole-plan review; then D2 battlefield forge + D3 living board. Open work is on the Agora board (category combat-map-visual).

features.title: D1 renderer prototype (Pixi ground + tokens + fog) — BUILT, 6 tasks reviewed; final whole-plan review pending (agora 31176b2a)
all-biome painted looks: TEN biomes; forest + desert REAL PAINTED ART (Caeora packs, generic loader); cave OPEN (forge or cave zip); other 7 procedural until packs — eyeball gate
combat demo moved to design preview (?step=battlemap) + window-frame layout fix + 3-col survives >=1024px
battlefield forge contract + road/river derived-map mock (D2) — eyeball gate
living-board storyboard (D3) — eyeball gate
migration + build in shippable slices (after approvals)

deps.why: the reskin is the current state the new renderer must match before exceeding
pillar 2 subsumes mechanical roads

topics.title: Combat-map shell: battlefield space, rails, and information priority
topics.sub: The tactical board now lets players independently hide the roster and command rails, giving the map room at laptop and desktop sizes without deleting combat information. First use opens both rails; later combats remember the player's last deliberate layout and bounded desktop widths. Mouse and keyboard resize handles preserve a battlefield share, and one reset restores the full default shell. A compact turn strip preserves the active actor, resource state, movement, and End Turn whenever the command rail is hidden. The remaining shell choice is whether map-focus mode should eventually expose favorite abilities. Added 2026-07-11 during the battle-map UI/UX continuation pass.

deps.why: the renderer can only feel map-first if the surrounding combat shell gives it predictable space and preserves tactical information intentionally

features.title: Independent roster and command-rail visibility controls shared by the real combat screen and design preview
Open first-use combat with both rails visible, then remember the player's last deliberate roster and command-rail layout
Use bounded, remembered desktop rail widths with mouse and keyboard resize handles, responsive battlefield caps, per-rail double-click reset, and one full panel reset
Keep a compact active-actor, action-economy, movement, and End Turn strip visible whenever the command rail is hidden
DECISION: Should map-focus mode remain status-only, expose three player-chosen favorite abilities, or open a compact radial ability picker on demand?

topics.title: Combat targeting: armed intent, validation, and cancellation
topics.sub: Target selection now has one shared intent HUD across the playable combat screen, design preview, 2D map, 3D map, and popped-out battlefield. The HUD states exactly what is armed and what valid choice the engine is waiting for, while the originating ability tile stays visibly pressed and can cancel the intent on a second click. The 2D map's Move / Attack strip now stays above painted terrain and names a genuinely ready direct attack instead of trusting ability-array order. Visible map and command cancellation plus Escape provide consistent exits without stealing Escape from focused text inputs. Invalid targets currently keep the ability armed and surface the validation reason; whether that forgiving retry behavior and the quick-Attack selection policy become permanent remain player-experience decisions. Added 2026-07-11 during the battle-map UI/UX continuation pass.

deps.why: targeting feedback must survive renderer changes so the player never loses track of an armed combat action

features.title: Show one shared armed-ability HUD with target instruction, cost, range, caster, follow-up, and description in every combat-map mode
Provide visible icon cancellation and Escape cancellation while preserving Escape inside focused text-editing controls
Keep the originating ability tile visibly pressed, close its redundant hover tooltip, and let a second click cancel the armed intent
Return stacked playable and design-preview layouts to the battlefield after an ability is armed
Keep the 2D Move / Attack toolbar above painted terrain, expose accessible pressed state, and select only a ready direct main-Action attack

DECISION: After an invalid target click, should targeting stay armed with an explanation or cancel immediately? Keep-armed retry is recommended; never snap to a nearby target the player did not choose.

DECISION: Should quick Attack remain the first ready direct attack in authored loadout order, remember the character's last explicitly used direct attack, or use a configurable favorite? Remembering the last explicit direct attack is recommended; never auto-pick highest damage.

topics.title: Procedural asset forge (owned stylized battle-map props)
topics.sub: code-drawn, seeded, owned art style for battle-map props as an alternative to found packs. Grew out of the cave-believability gap 2026-07-07: procedural drawers reach 'good stylized game art' (light upper-left + ink outlines throughout). BUILT: forge module (src/components/BattleMap/forge/) + design-preview showcase (?step=assetforge) with set selector / reroll / PNG export. Cave set done (ore rocks, crystal-ore nodes, chunky crystals, stalagmites, glowing mushrooms). NOT wired to the game yet — design surface only; same drawers can feed the painter's biome branches once a set is approved. Adding a set = one <name>Forge.ts + one registry entry.

features.title: cave set + design-preview showcase (?step=assetforge): rocks, crystal-ore nodes, chunky crystals, stalagmites, glow mushrooms; set selector / reroll / PNG export
refine cave set: stalagmite texture, crystal tips, cohesion pass (agora eab6558d)
wire forge cave set into painter cave biome — resolves cave-believability (decision-gated forge vs cave zip; agora eca9ab35)
expand to more sets (dungeon next; one <name>Forge.ts + one registry entry) (agora 9f21f450)

deps.why: the painted-pack look upgrade exposed the gap this fills; forge props would feed the same painter that stamps the Caeora packs

topics.title: Fights leave permanent marks on the world
topics.sub: battle scars written BACK to the world, not reset per fight: the scorched grove stays scorched next season, the destroyed bridge is gone until rebuilt (and the town sim reacts), bodies get graves, locals rename places. Combat becomes one of the things that writes world history. Brainstormed 2026-07-06 during the nextgen eyeball gate; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: needs the battlefield forge's canonical site identity (same place = same battlefield) so a scar has somewhere durable to live

topics.title: Terrain that fights back
topics.sub: elemental terrain simulation with REAL rules, not paint: fire spreads tile-to-tile through dry grass driven by wind, rain douses it, frost freezes the pond into crossable-but-slippery ground, felled trees become cover, mud bogs movement. The referee updates with the terrain, so casting fire in a dry forest is a genuine tactical decision. Brainstormed 2026-07-06; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: every terrain state is another layer the single-scene renderer can draw; the DOM grid could never afford it

topics.title: Elevation as a tactical dimension
topics.sub: painted relief (ridgelines, hollows, cliff edges, hand-drawn-map shading) with mechanical truth: high ground helps, falls hurt, hills block sight, ambush-from-above becomes real. The world already knows its elevations; combat isn't listening yet. Brainstormed 2026-07-06; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: pillar 2's battlefield forge already carries elevation samples from the worldforge cell — this makes them tactical and visible

topics.title: Fight chronicles (replay + retellings)
topics.sub: a finished fight is saved as a chronicle: an animated replay you can scrub like a short film, with the narrated log as captions — and NPCs retell garbled versions in taverns via the living-world rumor machinery. Your greatest fight becomes a rumor you overhear a year later. Brainstormed 2026-07-06; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: pillar 3's narrated log and cinematic turn animations are the raw material a chronicle replays

topics.title: Large set-piece battles
topics.sub: the renderer freed of one-DOM-node-per-tile can seat real set pieces: defend a village alongside its militia while townsfolk flee to their actual houses, thirty-combatant caravan ambushes, sieges. The living world provides the cast. Brainstormed 2026-07-06; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: hard perf prerequisite: chunked GPU rendering is what makes 30+ combatants and bigger boards affordable

topics.title: Enemy intent on the board
topics.sub: telegraph what enemies are winding up to do — subtle threat lines / wind-up markers for the goblin's next move, tactics-game style. Turns 'why did I die' into 'I saw it coming and gambled'. Brainstormed 2026-07-06; captured at Remy's pick.

deps.why: drawn on the tactical-overlay layer of the new renderer; needs the enemy AI to expose its chosen action a turn early

topics.title: Glossary structured content layer (typed model, one link primitive, build-time gate)
topics.sub: spec APPROVED 2026-07-06 (one bundle file; gate fails on everything day one, no grandfathered baseline; source files get fixed, not permanently auto-repaired): replace the regex + dangerouslySetInnerHTML string pipeline with a build-time compiler that parses each of the ~2,000 entries once into typed blocks, precomputes the cross-reference graph, and fails CI on broken links/malformed tables/content-less targets; one GlossaryLink primitive replaces five clickable-term implementations. Stretch (design-only): rules surface live game-state clauses via the graph. SLICE 1 DONE 2026-07-06: compiler+graph+gate live in glossary:rebuild and vitest; root-cause ingest pipe-corruption fixed; ~850 entries source-cleaned; gate green over all 1,571 entries. CROSS-SURFACE AUDIT 2026-07-07: spellbook/spell UI already clean (structured JSON, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML/markdown-at-render); only fragile render-time markdown left is a dev-only tool (PreviewMdLibrary, single terminal DOMPurify — not actually vulnerable). Down-payments landed: item registry now strips 5eTools {@item} tags at generation (0 leaked); party-condition chips (poisoned) now link to the live glossary entry via GlossaryTooltip instead of hardcoded copy (early slice-4).

features.title: compiler + content model + cross-reference graph + full source cleanup (hard CI gate, no UI change)
entry modal renders from typed blocks (dangerouslySetInnerHTML removed)
three-pane browser on the block renderer
GlossaryLink primitive unifies all five link surfaces (started: condition chips + skills reuse GlossaryTooltip)
string pipeline deleted + known-issues baseline burned to zero
live game-state rules layer over the link graph

topics.title: Human escalation channel
topics.sub: one fleet-wide queue for decisions only the human can make: `escalate "<q>" --options --blocking` posts an identity-stamped question; the master funnels open ones to Remy AS AskUserQuestion choices and routes answers back, waking the waiting agent via the proven Path C listener. Answered escalations = the fleet decision log. Specced 2026-07-06; not built

deps.why: Every escalation is stamped with the asker’s full provenance so Remy sees which agent/campaign/where-from is blocked — a bare handle is not enough to decide
The human’s answer wakes the waiting agent via the same Path C in-session listener that ping proved — escalation reuses that wake path rather than inventing one

features.title: escalation record + escalate command + escalations --open list (with tests)
master funnel: poll queue → present open blocking escalations via AskUserQuestion → record answer
answer-routing: wake the waiting agent (Path C) with the decision + dedup/fan-out identical asks

topics.title: Groq inference toggle
topics.sub: player-toggled Groq cloud text when Ollama is down; user-entered key in localStorage (no bundle leak); covers every Ollama text call

features.title: Key-handling the user picks: persistent localStorage / session-only / local-proxy (key never in the browser) — none in the bundle. Local-proxy path is now ALWAYS-ON in dev: the Vite dev server itself proxies /__groq/v1 (scripts/vite-plugins/groqProxyManager.ts), reading the key from Windows Credential Manager (AgentMatrix/Groq/GROQ_API_KEY) — same-origin, no separate process, no CORS, nothing to start by hand. Default proxy URL is now /__groq/v1; verified keyLoaded + proxy-ok live. Standalone tools/groq-proxy (npm run groq-proxy, :8787) still serves the same shape for non-Vite use
Groq OpenAI-compatible text provider adapting to the Ollama client return shapes
Provider router at the ollama-client facade (aiTextProvider ollama|groq)
Toggle on the Ollama-unavailable message: enter key + switch provider

Active-provider banner at the top of the pane: reads 'Currently active: Groq cloud (local proxy)' in sky, or 'Ollama (local model) — the default' in gray, so the pane reflects reality instead of always reading 'Ollama required'. Updates live on switch; verified both states

The whole Ollama explainer (what it is / what breaks without it / how to start it) is grouped in one bordered container with a collapse/expand toggle. Defaults expanded when Ollama is active, collapsed when Groq is on (the block is then noise). Conditional-render collapse after animated approaches proved brittle in this layout (grid 0fr floors at min-content; framer auto-height stalls; max-height transition never settles). Verified both defaults + toggle
CSP connect-src allows api.groq.com (Groq fetch was blocked); localhost:* already covers the proxy option

topics.title: Agora fleet coordination (epic)
topics.sub: the umbrella program that turns Agora’s peer pool into a legible, recoverable, wake-able, human-steerable fleet. Ties together identity + retrace + ping + escalation + master (+ frontier: ask-await, stall-detection, retire, attribution) into a 4-wave build order. Path C wake PROVEN 2026-07-06. Specced; build not started

features.title: Wave 1 Foundation: agent-identity + clean-exit/retire (legible fleet: whois/lineage/tree)
Wave 2 Recovery & reach: agent-retrace ∥ agent-ping (dead work recoverable; agents wake-able)
Wave 3 Coordination: ask-and-await → agent-escalation (agents ask each other + the human)
Wave 4 Oversight capstone: stall-detection + attribution + master-orchestrator

topics.title: Economy player-facing UX pass
topics.sub: The economy engine is built + wired into worldReducer (Steps 5b/5c), but has NO honest player surface: investment board, ledger, courier pouch, trade-route dashboard, and merchant modal are dev-menu-only, and most gold movements bypass the transaction framework (OPEN_MERCHANT has zero player dispatch sites). Goal: give the economy real in-game entry points so a player earns/spends/sees gold move through the framework. DECISION-GATED / supervised UX pass (Remy in the loop, mock-up + look-approve first) — handed off, not auto-built. Unblocks economy-ui G4/G8/G9 + the merchant-haggle discount persistence (needs merchantId threaded). Handover prompt in the link.

topics.title: LLM provider as a user configuration choice
topics.sub: Reframe (Remy 2026-07-09): the divergent Ollama model-fallback chains scattered across files (types/ollama.ts, ollama/taskProfiles.ts, useBiomeGenerator.ts hardcoded mistral/phi4-mini/gemma3, CompanionGenerator.ts) should NOT be a silent fallback — they should be a user-facing CONFIGURATION choice of LLM provider, by system + availability. Remy's own setup leverages local Ollama to avoid token/rate limits; other users may prefer a different LLM agent (Gemini/Groq/etc.) depending on their machine and access. Direction: make the provider (and its model) a user config, KEEP the no-fallback directive (fail honestly on the chosen provider, no silent model-swapping), and consolidate the currently-hardcoded divergent chains behind that one config. FIRST SLICE SHIPPED 2026-07-09 (behavior-preserving): new src/config/llmProviderConfig.ts centralizes all the scattered hardcoded model choices (global fallback chain, the 4 task-category lists, companion 'mistral:instruct', biome list) with defaults that byte-for-byte reproduce today's behavior; types/ollama.ts, taskProfiles.ts, CompanionGenerator.ts, useBiomeGenerator.ts now import from it; each chain carries a TODO(llm-provider-config) marker. Gate: tsc 635 (no regression), 8/8 config-default pins green. NEXT: the user-facing provider/model settings toggle + wiring the config to actually switch provider (aiProviderSettings.ts already holds the Groq/provider surface).

topics.title: Interior lighting on the live clock
topics.sub: DONE 2026-07-09. Windows and the hearth now light and darken with the game clock, instead of freezing at the hour you walked in. The whole day is baked once as a schedule; the render side reads it live. Proof: 0 lit windows at noon, 26 at dusk, dark again by night — same building, clock moved.

deps.why: Windows and hearths are fixtures of the v2 building shell; the live clock re-times the hearth-lit hours the living overlay already bakes.

features.title: Windows and hearths light live from the daily schedule
One shared building placement transform (no hand-copied drift)
Live in-engine day and night eyeball

topics.title: Interior occupants move by the hour
topics.sub: DONE 2026-07-09. Each family member now moves through the house hour by hour on the live clock. Proof: ~1581 people home at night vs ~892 at midday, shifting as the clock advances. v1 snaps between spots; walking between them is the follow-up below.

deps.why: Consumes the living overlay's per-hour occupancy stations; without the household brief and station schedule there is no one to move.
Shares the baked 24-hour schedule and the one plan-feet-to-scene transform; built alongside so both read the same live clock.

features.title: Family stands at hourly stations in 3D, live
Occupants walk between stations
Merge interior occupants into the street agent sim

topics.title: Servants as real NPCs
topics.sub: Today a servant is only a tag on a house's design brief: it shapes which rooms get built, then never becomes a person (blindspot finding #2 — servant slots drive room programming but place no body). Promote servants to real household NPCs with a role in the household, a daily schedule, and a body that stands and moves like any resident. This is an NPC/roster system, not a field on a building — it is why the live-clock work split occupants away from building fixtures. Direction set by Remy 2026-07-09; not yet specced.

deps.why: A servant needs the interior figure layer to appear and move; servants ride the same per-hour occupant system as residents.
Servant slots already exist in the v2 household brief and shape the plan; this gives those slots a real person.

topics.title: AI-arbitrated ritual casting
topics.sub: Remy's design 2026-07-09: rituals become a REAL feature but heavily AI-arbitrated and state-aware, NOT a progress bar the player babysits. A ritual is a background narrative process; drama comes from who is around and what is happening. SAFE place -> quiet background dice roll; interruptions are social + personality-driven (a companion who doesn't get magic / isn't aware of the ritual may cause a funny distraction -> concentration roll; a quiet/shy NPC less likely, a comedian/low-INT more likely; a companion who knows the player may head off the disruptor, but only if >1 companion present). HIGHER THREAT place -> danger-driven: waylaid by enemies, or sound/visual signals surfaced via passive perception ('you hear/see X' -> player may abort); companions roll passive perception too (background per-event DC -> auto pass/fail); noticing NPCs choose investigate / sound alarm / dismiss, driven by paranoid vs careless traits. Needs: awareness model (who's near + who knows), NPC personality traits, a NEW flexible per-event DC mechanic, AI arbitration (Ollama-gated, respects no-fallback/provider-config), and the existing ritual engine's completion payoff + backlash (currently stubs). Background clock already works. Needs a brainstorm pass then phased build. FIRST PREREQUISITE BUILT 2026-07-09 (no-decision, reusable): the flexible per-event detection mechanic — new src/systems/perception/eventDetection.ts with EventDC + difficulty bands (5e DC ladder, tunable), passivePerceptionOf (reuses the existing calculatePassiveScore; supports PC compute + NPC passivePerception field), and resolveEventDetection (passive auto-detect >= DC, optional seeded active roll, no-fallback throw). Gate: tsc 635, 17/17 tests. This is the 'companions roll passive perception (background DC -> auto pass/fail)' piece. REMAINING pieces are ritual-specific arbitration that need the brainstorm: awareness model (who's near + who knows — the memory factKey from the just-shipped merge feeds 'who knows'), personality->interruption/reaction mapping, AI narration, cast-trigger + command routing, real completion payoff, backlash.

deps.why: Ritual interruption/reaction arbitration reads NPC personality (shy/comedian/paranoid/careless). Remy 2026-07-09: personality is broader than rituals, split off as its own foundational NPC-behavior track; rituals consume it.

topics.title: NPC memory: merge or keep separate
topics.sub: Two NPC-memory systems exist. LIVE: world-state NpcMemory (types/world.ts) - disposition/suspicion/plain facts/goals, filled by talking/haggling/crime/gossip, ~20 consumers, drives recruitment + dossier + prices. DORMANT: richer NPCMemory (types/memory.ts) - timestamped interactions, emotion, per-fact confidence, importance, decay; self-labeled orphan, filled only in tests. world.ts is already half-extended with the richer type's optional fields. DETERMINATION 2026-07-09 = MERGE (evidence-backed): they are the SAME system forked, not separate purposes. docs/projects/memory/GAPS.md already tracks this as G1 (schema-normalization: pick one canonical shape) + G2 (retire the orphan MemorySystem); the richer NPCMemory was the intended structured upgrade (generator TODO: 'Replace NpcMemory with the structured NPCMemory when memory models merge'), parked ~6 weeks with ZERO live writes and its one read fed a hardcoded-empty memory; world.ts was deliberately half-widened 2026-07-03 to hold the richer fields (typed loosely) awaiting exactly this call. Migration sketch ready: extend KnownFact with optional richer fields (add a semantic factKey — which is what makes 'companion knows player is ritual-casting' a reliable query), finish the world.ts typing, repoint the decay/format helpers onto the canonical shape, retire MemorySystem, save/load backfill. Low blast radius (~20 live consumers keep working; richer lane has ~0 to preserve). MERGE EXECUTED + gate-green 2026-07-09: canonical = live NpcMemory; KnownFact extended with optional factKey/confidence/significance + widened source; NpcMemory half-merge fields tightened (Interaction[]/number/Record<string,number>); NPCMemory now aliases the canonical type; orphan MemorySystem.ts + test deleted; memoryUtils helpers repointed; save/load backfills from strength. tsc 635 (no regression), 28/28 memory/npc/gemini tests green. Closes memory G1 + G2. TAIL CLOSED 2026-07-10: the 3 stale .d.ts twins (memory.d.ts/world.d.ts/index.d.ts) deleted; verified not build-generated (tsconfig noEmit; node-tsconfig emitDeclarationOnly scopes only vite.config+named scripts) and not needed by any tsconfig include; sole consumer test:types repointed from index.d.ts to index.ts; tsc -b still 635 (byte-identical error set). Broader twin sweep spun off as types-decl-twin-hygiene.

topics.title: Plan-map status on the roadmap + cross-tool nav

topics.title: NPC personality system
topics.sub: Foundational NPC-behavior track, split off the ritual track by Remy 2026-07-09 ('this is more about NPC behavior, in particular their personality traits, at first'). Today characters carry only a free-text traits: string[] + real ability scores; there is NO structured personality model. Build one that drives how NPCs behave: whether a companion interrupts the player (shy/reserved less likely, boisterous/comedian more likely), how an NPC reacts to a noticed danger (paranoid investigates/alarms, careless dismisses), and more broadly dialogue tone, party dynamics, and the living-world sim. Design decisions still OPEN (brainstorm redirected here before the model was picked): numeric personality axes (Sociability/Caution/Curiosity/Impulsiveness/Confidence, behaviors derive from thresholds) vs a fixed trait-tag enum vs Big-Five. Consumed by [[ai-arbitrated-ritual-casting]] (interruption/reaction arbitration) and feeds NPC memory ([[npc-memory-reconciliation]], now merged). Needs its own brainstorm → spec → build.

topics.title: Stale src/types .d.ts twin cleanup
topics.sub: src/types holds hand-committed .d.ts files that shadow-duplicate their .ts source; 45 of 50 .d.ts have a live .ts twin. TypeScript resolves the .ts over the .d.ts, so the twins are invisible today (0 tsc errors) but latent: if anything ever imports an explicit .d path or a build starts emitting them, they resurface retired shapes. Confirmed NOT build-generated (tsconfig noEmit; tsconfig.node emitDeclarationOnly scopes only vite.config + a few named scripts, never src/types). FIRST 3 CLEARED 2026-07-10 (the ones the NPC-memory merge desynced: memory/world/index — see [[npc-memory-reconciliation]]); tsc -b held at 635 byte-identical. REMAINING: (1) audit the other ~42 twins, delete the ones that are pure stale duplicates (some may be legitimately hand-authored ambient decls with no .ts — the 5 .d-only files — leave those), tsc-diff each batch; (2) test:types is currently RED anyway — 18 pre-existing ConditionName drift errors in src/types/__tests__/spells.test-d.ts, not run in CI — either fix the spells declaration drift and gate it in CI, or retire the script. Low blast radius, parallelizable by disjoint file batches.

deps.why: the memory merge exposed the twin-shadowing trap and cleared the first 3; this sweep generalizes it to the rest of src/types
